Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Tmux Session/Window Search Improvements

## Context
The setup already has `_tss_function` (session picker, `prefix+S` / `tss`) and `tmux-sessionx` (`prefix+o`).
The gaps are:
1. `_tss_function` is blind — no preview of what windows are in a session, and no way to kill a session from the picker.
2. There is no cross-session **window** fuzzy picker — no way to jump directly to a named window by name across all sessions.
3. No quick last-session toggle keybinding in tmux.

## Improvements

### 1. Enhance `_tss_function` — preview + kill binding

Add two fzf flags:
- `--preview 'tmux list-windows -F "#I: #W" -t {} 2>/dev/null'` — shows windows of hovered session
- `--bind 'ctrl-x:execute-silent(tmux kill-session -t {})+abort'` — kills the selected session and exits

Only the `fzf` call on line 13 changes; all session logic stays the same.

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish`

```fish
  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf \
    --prompt="session> " \
    --height=40% \
    --preview='tmux list-windows -F "#I: #W" -t {} 2>/dev/null' \
    --bind='ctrl-x:execute-silent(tmux kill-session -t {})+abort')
```

---

### 2. New `_twf_function` — cross-session window fuzzy picker

Lists every window across every session as `session:index  window_name`. Selecting one switches to that session then that window.

**File to create:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_twf_function.fish`

```fish
function _twf_function --description "Fuzzy-pick any window across all sessions"
  set -l selected (tmux list-windows -a -F '#{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}' 2>/dev/null \
    | fzf --prompt="window> " --height=40% \
          --preview='set t (string split "  " {})[1]; tmux list-panes -t $t -F "#P: #{pane_current_command}  #{pane_current_path}" 2>/dev/null')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[1]   # "session:index"
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end
```

Register in **`home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix`**:
- Add `"_twf_function"` to the `xdg.configFile` function list (alphabetical between `_tss_function` and `_two_function`)
- Add `twf = "_twf_function";` to `shellAbbrs`

---

### 3. `tmux.conf` — last-session toggle + window picker bind

Add two lines to **`home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`** in the bindings section:

```
# Last session (quick flip)
bind Tab switch-client -l

# Cross-session window picker
bind W run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _twf_function'"
```

`Tab` is currently unbound. `W` is currently unbound (lowercase `w` is default select-window by index, not bound here).

---

## Files to Modify/Create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish` | Add preview + ctrl-x kill to fzf call |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_twf_function.fish` | **create** — cross-session window picker |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` | Add `_twf_function` to list + `twf` abbr |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `bind Tab switch-client -l` and `bind W` |

## Verification

1. `tss` → hover over a session → right panel shows numbered windows
2. `tss` → hover over a session → press `ctrl-x` → session is killed, picker closes
3. `twf` (or `prefix+W` inside tmux) → lists all windows from all sessions → select one → lands in the right session+window
4. Inside tmux, press `prefix+Tab` → switches to the previously active session instantly

function _tss_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  # Build candidate list: default sessions first, then any extra existing sessions
  set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set -l candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)

  if test -n "$selected"
    if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    else if test "$selected" = work
      set -l restore (tmux list-keys 2>/dev/null | string match -rg '(/\S+/resurrect/scripts/restore\.sh)')
      set -l restore $restore[1]
      if test -n "$restore"
        tmux run-shell "$restore"
      end
      if tmux has-session -t work 2>/dev/null
        if test -n "$TMUX"
          tmux switch-client -t work
        else
          tmux attach-session -t work
        end
      else
        tmuxinator start work
      end
    else if contains $selected $default_sessions
      tmuxinator start "$selected"
    else
      tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    end
  end
end

# From: https://github.com/caarlos0/dotfiles/blob/f9d985c3e8817f8fca133d908a910f173ea933d6/modules/tmux/tmux.conf
# License: MIT

# Color Mode
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
setenv -g COLORTERM "truecolor"

set -s escape-time 0
setw -g mouse on

# Fix SSH agent after reconnecting
# https://blog.testdouble.com/posts/2016-11-18-reconciling-tmux-and-ssh-agent-forwarding/
set -g update-environment "DISPLAY SSH_ASKPASS SSH_AGENT_PID SSH_CONNECTION WINDOWID XAUTHORITY WAYLAND_DISPLAY SWAYSOCK"
setenv -g SSH_AUTH_SOCK $HOME/.ssh/ssh_auth_sock

# Ensure window index numbers get reordered on delete.
set-option -g renumber-windows on

# Auto rename tmux window to current cwd
set-option -g status-interval 1
set-option -g automatic-rename on
set-option -g automatic-rename-format '#{b:pane_current_path}'

# Vim-mode copy/paste
set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'

# Open splits and windows in the current folder
bind u split-window -l 30% -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind i split-window -l 40% -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Repeat time
set-option -g repeat-time 1000

# Clipboard
set -g set-clipboard on

# Status bar
set -g status "on"
set -g status-justify "left"
set -g status-left-length "100"
set -g status-right-length "100"
set -g status-left ""
set -g status-left-style NONE
set -g status-right-style NONE

# Reload tmux config
bind t source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

# Default shell
set -gu default-command
set -g default-shell "$SHELL"

# Rename session and window
bind r command-prompt -I "#{window_name}" "rename-window '%%'"
bind R command-prompt -I "#{session_name}" "rename-session '%%'"

# Copy text on select
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "reattach-to-user-namespace pbcopy"

# Switch window
bind , previous-window
bind . next-window

# Move pane bindings
bind-key h   select-pane -L
bind-key j   select-pane -D
bind-key k   select-pane -U
bind-key l   select-pane -R

# Swap window bindings
bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1
bind-key -r ">" swap-window -d -t +1

# Resize pane bindings
bind-key -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind-key -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind-key -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind-key -r L resize-pane -R 5

# Split pane bindings
bind-key "|" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "\\" split-window -fh -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "-" split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "_" split-window -fv -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "%" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Yank pane history to clipboard
bind y run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -pS - | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Full pane history copied to clipboard"'
bind v run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -p | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Visible pane copied to clipboard"'

# Pane number indicator
set -g display-panes-colour colour233
set -g display-panes-active-colour colour245

# Clock mode
set -g clock-mode-colour colour39
set -g clock-mode-style 24

# =============================================================================
# Status bar — clean green theme
# =============================================================================
set -g status-style "bg=colour22,fg=colour255"
set -g status-left "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #S #[bg=colour22,fg=colour28] "
set -g status-right "#[fg=colour250]#{?#{pane_current_command},#{pane_current_command},} #[fg=colour28]| #[fg=colour255]%Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "
set -g status-left-length 30
set -g status-right-length 60

# Window status
set -g window-status-format " #I:#W "
set -g window-status-current-format "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #I:#W "
set -g window-status-separator ""

# Pane border
set -g pane-border-style "fg=colour238"
set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=colour82,bold"

# Message style
set -g message-style "bg=colour28,fg=colour255"

# =============================================================================
# Plugin configuration
# =============================================================================
# Resurrect + Continuum (session persistence)
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'

# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)
set -g @thumbs-key Space

# Sessionx (session manager)
set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'

# Tmux sessionizer (fzf session picker)
bind S run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tss_function'"

# Extrakto (text extraction)
set -g @extrakto_key 'tab'

function _tss_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  # Build candidate list: default sessions first, then any extra existing sessions
  set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set -l candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)

  if test -n "$selected"
    if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    else if test "$selected" = work
      set -l restore (tmux list-keys 2>/dev/null | string match -rg '(/\S+/resurrect/scripts/restore\.sh)')
      set -l restore $restore[1]
      if test -n "$restore"
        tmux run-shell "$restore"
      end
      if tmux has-session -t work 2>/dev/null
        if test -n "$TMUX"
          tmux switch-client -t work
        else
          tmux attach-session -t work
        end
      else
        tmuxinator start work
      end
    else if contains $selected $default_sessions
      tmuxinator start "$selected"
    else
      tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    end
  end
end

function _twf_function --description "Fuzzy-pick any window across all sessions"
  set -l selected (tmux list-windows -a -F '#{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}' 2>/dev/null \
    | fzf --prompt="window> " --height=40% \
          --preview='set t (string split "  " {})[1]; tmux list-panes -t $t -F "#P: #{pane_current_command}  #{pane_current_path}" 2>/dev/null')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[1]   # "session:index"
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end

# Plan: Tmux Session/Window Search Improvements

## Context
The setup already has `_tss_function` (session picker, `prefix+S` / `tss`) and `tmux-sessionx` (`prefix+o`).
The gaps are:
1. `_tss_function` is blind — no preview of what windows are in a session, and no way to kill a session from the picker.
2. There is no cross-session **window** fuzzy picker — no way to jump directly to a named window by name across all sessions.
3. No quick last-session toggle keybinding in tmux.

## Improvements

### 1. Enhance `_tss_function` — preview + kill binding

Add two fzf flags:
- `--preview 'tmux list-windows -F "#I: #W" -t {} 2>/dev/null'` — shows windows of hovered session
- `--bind 'ctrl-x:execute-silent(tmux kill-session -t {})+abort'` — kills the selected session and exits

Only the `fzf` call on line 13 changes; all session logic stays the same.

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish`

```fish
  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf \
    --prompt="session> " \
    --height=40% \
    --preview='tmux list-windows -F "#I: #W" -t {} 2>/dev/null' \
    --bind='ctrl-x:execute-silent(tmux kill-session -t {})+abort')
```

---

### 2. New `_twf_function` — cross-session window fuzzy picker

Lists every window across every session as `session:index  window_name`. Selecting one switches to that session then that window.

**File to create:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_twf_function.fish`

```fish
function _twf_function --description "Fuzzy-pick any window across all sessions"
  set -l selected (tmux list-windows -a -F '#{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}' 2>/dev/null \
    | fzf --prompt="window> " --height=40% \
          --preview='set t (string split "  " {})[1]; tmux list-panes -t $t -F "#P: #{pane_current_command}  #{pane_current_path}" 2>/dev/null')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[1]   # "session:index"
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end
```

Register in **`home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix`**:
- Add `"_twf_function"` to the `xdg.configFile` function list (alphabetical between `_tss_function` and `_two_function`)
- Add `twf = "_twf_function";` to `shellAbbrs`

---

### 3. `tmux.conf` — last-session toggle + window picker bind

Add two lines to **`home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`** in the bindings section:

```
# Last session (quick flip)
bind Tab switch-client -l

# Cross-session window picker
bind W run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _twf_function'"
```

`Tab` is currently unbound. `W` is currently unbound (lowercase `w` is default select-window by index, not bound here).

---

## Files to Modify/Create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish` | Add preview + ctrl-x kill to fzf call |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_twf_function.fish` | **create** — cross-session window picker |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` | Add `_twf_function` to list + `twf` abbr |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `bind Tab switch-client -l` and `bind W` |

## Verification

1. `tss` → hover over a session → right panel shows numbered windows
2. `tss` → hover over a session → press `ctrl-x` → session is killed, picker closes
3. `twf` (or `prefix+W` inside tmux) → lists all windows from all sessions → select one → lands in the right session+window
4. Inside tmux, press `prefix+Tab` → switches to the previously active session instantly

# Plan: Tmux Session History + `two` Resurrect Fix

## Context

Three related improvements to the tmux workflow:

1. **`_two_function` resurrect gap** — `_two_function` only runs resurrect restore when the work session does _not_ exist. `_tss_function` (work branch) runs restore _first_, then checks existence. `_two_function` should match that behavior so reconnecting always replays any saved state.

2. **Session history log** — No persistent record of which sessions/windows were active. Adding a 30-second background snapshot to a log file enables "where was I?" lookups via fzf.

3. **`_tsh_function` search** — A fish function to fzf-search the history log and optionally jump to a still-existing session/window.

---

## Changes

### 1. Fix `_two_function` — always resurrect first

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish`

Move the resurrect restore call to _before_ the session-existence check:

```fish
function _two_function --description "Attach to tmux work session"
  set -l restore (tmux list-keys 2>/dev/null | string match -rg '(/\S+/resurrect/scripts/restore\.sh)')
  set -l restore $restore[1]
  if test -n "$restore"
    tmux run-shell "$restore"
  end

  if tmux has-session -t work 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t work
    else
      tmux attach-session -t work
    end
  else
    tmuxinator start work
  end
end
```

---

### 2. Session logger script

**File to add via `home.file`:** `~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh`

Declare in **`home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix`** using `home.file`:

```nix
home.file.".config/tmux/session-logger.sh" = {
  executable = true;
  text = ''
    #!/usr/bin/env sh
    mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux
    LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
    while true; do
      sleep 30
      tmux list-windows -a \
        -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
        >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null
      # Trim to last 10 000 lines to cap file size
      if [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG")" -gt 10000 ]; then
        tail -n 10000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"
      fi
    done
  '';
};
```

Start it when the tmux server boots — add to **`home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`**:

```
# Persistent session history logger (starts once per server boot)
set-hook -g 'server-startup' 'run-shell -b "~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh"'
```

---

### 3. New `_tsh_function` — search session history

**File to create:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish`

```fish
function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  # Field 2 is "session:window_index"
  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end
```

Register in **`home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix`**:
- Add `"_tsh_function"` to the `xdg.configFile` function list (alphabetically between `_tss_function` and `_twf_function`)
- Add `tsh = "_tsh_function";` to `shellAbbrs`

---

## Files to Modify / Create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish` | Move resurrect to before session-existence check |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Add `home.file` for `session-logger.sh` |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `server-startup` hook to launch logger |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish` | **create** — session history search |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` | Add `_tsh_function` to list + `tsh` abbr |

---

## Verification

1. `two` with no work session → resurrect runs first, work session restored or tmuxinator starts
2. `two` with existing work session → resurrect runs silently, then attaches (no harm if nothing to restore)
3. After tmux server boots, `~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log` starts populating after 30s
4. `tsh` → fzf shows history newest-first → select an entry → switches to still-existing session/window; prints error for dead sessions
5. Log file stays under 10 000 lines after many saves

# Plan: Tmux Session/Window Search Improvements

## Context
The setup already has `_tss_function` (session picker, `prefix+S` / `tss`) and `tmux-sessionx` (`prefix+o`).
The gaps are:
1. `_tss_function` is blind — no preview of what windows are in a session, and no way to kill a session from the picker.
2. There is no cross-session **window** fuzzy picker — no way to jump directly to a named window by name across all sessions.
3. No quick last-session toggle keybinding in tmux.

## Improvements

### 1. Enhance `_tss_function` — preview + kill binding

Add two fzf flags:
- `--preview 'tmux list-windows -F "#I: #W" -t {} 2>/dev/null'` — shows windows of hovered session
- `--bind 'ctrl-x:execute-silent(tmux kill-session -t {})+abort'` — kills the selected session and exits

Only the `fzf` call on line 13 changes; all session logic stays the same.

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish`

```fish
  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf \
    --prompt="session> " \
    --height=40% \
    --preview='tmux list-windows -F "#I: #W" -t {} 2>/dev/null' \
    --bind='ctrl-x:execute-silent(tmux kill-session -t {})+abort')
```

---

### 2. New `_twf_function` — cross-session window fuzzy picker

Lists every window across every session as `session:index  window_name`. Selecting one switches to that session then that window.

**File to create:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_twf_function.fish`

```fish
function _twf_function --description "Fuzzy-pick any window across all sessions"
  set -l selected (tmux list-windows -a -F '#{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}' 2>/dev/null \
    | fzf --prompt="window> " --height=40% \
          --preview='set t (string split "  " {})[1]; tmux list-panes -t $t -F "#P: #{pane_current_command}  #{pane_current_path}" 2>/dev/null')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[1]   # "session:index"
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end
```

Register in **`home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix`**:
- Add `"_twf_function"` to the `xdg.configFile` function list (alphabetical between `_tss_function` and `_two_function`)
- Add `twf = "_twf_function";` to `shellAbbrs`

---

### 3. `tmux.conf` — last-session toggle + window picker bind

Add two lines to **`home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`** in the bindings section:

```
# Last session (quick flip)
bind Tab switch-client -l

# Cross-session window picker
bind W run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _twf_function'"
```

`Tab` is currently unbound. `W` is currently unbound (lowercase `w` is default select-window by index, not bound here).

---

## Files to Modify/Create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish` | Add preview + ctrl-x kill to fzf call |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_twf_function.fish` | **create** — cross-session window picker |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` | Add `_twf_function` to list + `twf` abbr |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `bind Tab switch-client -l` and `bind W` |

## Verification

1. `tss` → hover over a session → right panel shows numbered windows
2. `tss` → hover over a session → press `ctrl-x` → session is killed, picker closes
3. `twf` (or `prefix+W` inside tmux) → lists all windows from all sessions → select one → lands in the right session+window
4. Inside tmux, press `prefix+Tab` → switches to the previously active session instantly

# Plan: Tmux Session History + `two` Resurrect Fix

## Context

Three related improvements to the tmux workflow:

1. **`_two_function` resurrect gap** — `_two_function` only runs resurrect restore when the work session does _not_ exist. `_tss_function` (work branch) runs restore _first_, then checks existence. `_two_function` should match that behavior so reconnecting always replays any saved state.

2. **Session history log** — No persistent record of which sessions/windows were active. Adding a 30-second background snapshot to a log file enables "where was I?" lookups via fzf.

3. **`_tsh_function` search** — A fish function to fzf-search the history log and optionally jump to a still-existing session/window.

---

## Changes

### 1. Fix `_two_function` — always resurrect first

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish`

Move the resurrect restore call to _before_ the session-existence check:

```fish
function _two_function --description "Attach to tmux work session"
  set -l restore (tmux list-keys 2>/dev/null | string match -rg '(/\S+/resurrect/scripts/restore\.sh)')
  set -l restore $restore[1]
  if test -n "$restore"
    tmux run-shell "$restore"
  end

  if tmux has-session -t work 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t work
    else
      tmux attach-session -t work
    end
  else
    tmuxinator start work
  end
end
```

---

### 2. Session logger script

**File to add via `home.file`:** `~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh`

Declare in **`home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix`** using `home.file`:

```nix
home.file.".config/tmux/session-logger.sh" = {
  executable = true;
  text = ''
    #!/usr/bin/env sh
    mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux
    LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
    while true; do
      sleep 30
      tmux list-windows -a \
        -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
        >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null
      # Trim to last 10 000 lines to cap file size
      if [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG")" -gt 10000 ]; then
        tail -n 10000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"
      fi
    done
  '';
};
```

Start it when the tmux server boots — add to **`home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`**:

```
# Persistent session history logger (starts once per server boot)
set-hook -g 'server-startup' 'run-shell -b "~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh"'
```

---

### 3. New `_tsh_function` — search session history

**File to create:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish`

```fish
function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  # Field 2 is "session:window_index"
  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end
```

Register in **`home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix`**:
- Add `"_tsh_function"` to the `xdg.configFile` function list (alphabetically between `_tss_function` and `_twf_function`)
- Add `tsh = "_tsh_function";` to `shellAbbrs`

---

## Files to Modify / Create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish` | Move resurrect to before session-existence check |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Add `home.file` for `session-logger.sh` |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `server-startup` hook to launch logger |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish` | **create** — session history search |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` | Add `_tsh_function` to list + `tsh` abbr |

---

## Verification

1. `two` with no work session → resurrect runs first, work session restored or tmuxinator starts
2. `two` with existing work session → resurrect runs silently, then attaches (no harm if nothing to restore)
3. After tmux server boots, `~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log` starts populating after 30s
4. `tsh` → fzf shows history newest-first → select an entry → switches to still-existing session/window; prints error for dead sessions
5. Log file stays under 10 000 lines after many saves
